# Methods

## Exploratory analyses

Weight-at-size are presented for the list of taxa in Table \@ref(tab:tabletaxa). 
For each taxon, a multi-panel figure showing the relationship between observed lengths and weights are provided. 
For taxa where sex-specific information is available, the weight-at-size relationships are presented separately for each sex. 
For taxa where size classes are used in the modelling, the weight-at-size relationships are presented separately for each size class. 


## Allometric function

The weight-at-size relationship used is the following:
\begin{equation}
W = \alpha L^{\beta}
\end{equation}
where $W$ is observed weight in grams, $L$ is the length in centimeters, and $a$ and $b$ are the allometric parameters to be estimated. 
It is common to log transform this relationship to make it linear:
\begin{equation}
log(W) = log(\alpha) +  \beta log(L)
\end{equation}
The parameter $log(\alpha)$ is referred to as the "$a$" parameter, and $\beta$ is referred to as the "$b$" parameter. 

Individual fish observations of length-weight pairs are used to estimate model parameters. 

## Mixed effects models

To allow for sex, size and year effects on the weight-at-size relationship, a mixed effects framework is adopted to analyse the available individual observations of length-weight pairs. 
The models considered appear in Table \@ref(tab:tablemodels). 
Models are implemented using the *glmmTMB* R package [@McGillycuddy-etal-2025] using R 4.6.0 [@cite-R].

## Southern Gulf of St. Lawrence ecosystem survey

Observations of fish and invertebrate taxa collected during the annual southern Gulf of St. Lawrence September survey are used to obtain taxon-specific estimates of the length-weight relationship. 

The taxa used in the analyses appear in Table \@ref(tab:tabletaxa).

### Model selection


### Bias and precision of predicted weights 

To determine the appropriateness of fitted models in predicting weight-at-size, residual plots are presented.


### Annual update of length-weight parameter estimates

The analyses presented herein are encapsulated in an R script that allows for an annual update of the estimated values for each taxon. 

## Comparison with estimates obtained from SAS 
A SAS script was developed by scientists at Fisheries and Oceans Canada to estimate the parameters of the relationship between weight and length. 
This script manually removed outliers for different taxa and also assigns each taxon to the type of estimates that will be calculated. 
The values derived from this script can be found in Table \@ref(tab:SASestimates) (Appendix \@ref(app:first-appendix))
